Title: Clock protein LHY targets SNAT1 and negatively regulates the biosynthesis of melatonin in Hypericum perforatum

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: WT laboratory line of H. perforatum; Col-0/OE-HpSNAT1 Arabidopsis for RNA-seq context
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acetyl group to serotonin to form N-acetylserotonin, a direct precursor of melatonin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step supported at transcriptional level in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rhythmic in H. perforatum seedlings/leaves under 12L/12D; peaks at ZT4 and persists under constant light
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rhythmic in H. perforatum; positively correlated with melatonin accumulation; Arabidopsis AtSNAT is repressed in HpLHY overexpression lines especially at ZT16
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes melatonin accumulation; reduced HpSNAT1 expression/protein associates with lower melatonin, and elevated expression associates with higher melatonin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR time-course; RNA-seq of OE-HpSNAT1 vs WT Arabidopsis; Western blot of HpSNAT1 in hairy roots; LC-MS melatonin profiling; promoter binding/regulation assays via Y1H, EMSA, dual-luciferase through upstream regulator HpLHY
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Growth regulation, antioxidant activity, and stress-response associated metabolite in plants
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves/seedlings of H. perforatum measured across diel cycle; hairy roots and regenerated plants also quantified
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 12 h light/12 h dark photoperiod; continuous light; sampling at ZT4 and ZT16
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native HpSNAT1 promoter (1.5 kb) in reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of Arabidopsis WT Col-0 and OE-HpSNAT1 at ZT4 and ZT16; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HpSNAT1 expression oscillates with a 24-hour rhythm in H. perforatum and peaks at ZT4, matching melatonin rhythm. 2. HpLHY directly binds the EE motif in the HpSNAT1 promoter and represses its expression. 3. HpSNAT1 transcript and protein levels positively correlate with melatonin content in HpLHY overexpression and knockout lines.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A useful melatonin-biosynthesis target for metabolic engineering; increasing HpSNAT1 expression or preventing HpLHY-mediated repression could elevate melatonin, but diel regulation should be considered
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although Arabidopsis OE-HpSNAT1 RNA-seq was used to infer circadian linkage, the principal causal validation in this paper concerns HpSNAT1 regulation in H. perforatum.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MYB-related transcription factor; LHY/CCA1-type clock protein with MYB DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: H. perforatum WT laboratory line; Arabidopsis lhy-11 cca1-21 in Ws background
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: HpSNAT1 promoter EE motif (AAATATCT); AtSNAT expression is also suppressed in Arabidopsis overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly represses HpSNAT1 transcription by binding the Evening Element in its promoter; suppresses AtSNAT expression in Arabidopsis complementation lines
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Circadian clock-linked negative regulator that is decisive for diel melatonin accumulation pattern
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in circadian feedback loop as an LHY clock component; specific melatonin feedback not established
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Mainly nucleus; minor signal in cytoplasm or cell membrane region
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in stems, low in flowers; rhythmic under 12L/12D with expression opposite to HpSNAT1, lowest at ZT8-ZT12 and increasing from ZT12, peaking late night/dawn-associated
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In Arabidopsis OE lines, HpLHY transcript is higher at ZT16 than ZT4; in H. perforatum its rhythm is opposite to melatonin/HpSNAT1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces melatonin accumulation by repressing SNAT expression; overexpression lowers melatonin, while knockout increases melatonin and disrupts rhythm
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast cDNA library screen/Y1H; rotational Y1H with promoter fragments; EMSA; transient dual-luciferase assay; subcellular localization; overexpression in Arabidopsis lhy cca1 mutant; overexpression and CRISPR-Cas9 knockout in H. perforatum hairy roots and regenerated plants; RT-qPCR; Western blot; LC-MS melatonin quantification
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Growth regulator, broad-spectrum antioxidant, and stress-related metabolite
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: H. perforatum seedlings/leaves, hairy roots, and regenerated plants
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 12 h light/12 h dark photoperiod; continuous light; diel sampling at 2-h intervals or ZT4/ZT16
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; native HpSNAT1 promoter in Y1H and dual-LUC assays
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/DEG and KEGG analyses comparing WT and OE-HpSNAT1 Arabidopsis; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HpLHY was identified from a yeast cDNA library as a protein that binds the HpSNAT1 promoter. 2. HpLHY directly binds the EE motif (AAATATCT) in the P1 region of the HpSNAT1 promoter and represses promoter activity. 3. HpLHY overexpression decreases HpSNAT1/AtSNAT expression and melatonin accumulation, whereas HpLHY knockout elevates melatonin and perturbs its rhythm in H. perforatum.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing or downregulating HpLHY could be used to increase melatonin accumulation in H. perforatum; however, precise tuning rather than constitutive overexpression is advisable because this clock regulator strongly affects growth and rhythmicity
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression causes growth retardation, stunted growth, reduced biomass, delayed callus/bud/root differentiation, and abnormal/disrupted melatonin rhythms
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: HpLHY lacks self-activation in yeast. H. perforatum appears to contain a single CCA1/LHY-like gene. Knockout increases melatonin but also abolishes normal melatonin phase rhythm under 12L/12D.
